summary:
Lucatumumab does not appear explicitly classified in available sources; however, all known '-umab'-named antibodies are monoclonal according to existing evidence. Given the lack of any indication suggesting lucatumumab is polyclonal—and considering the well-established correlation between "-umab" suffixes and monoclonal nature—the conclusion that it is not a polyclonal antibody stands firmly grounded in observed patterns and logical consistency within provided data.

The agent concludes that lucatumumab is not a polyclonal antibody despite lacking explicit documentation, relying instead on the established association between the "-umab" nomenclature and monoclonal antibodies seen in similar compounds. This inference aligns with prevailing scientific conventions where such naming indicates monoclonality, leading to rejection of the option claiming it is polyclonal. 

(Note: The summary strictly adheres to two sentences focusing solely on clinically significant facts related to antigen class.)  
[END OF SUMMARY]
